title: The End of the Tunnel — Case File 2: The Missing Ledger
description: Case File 2 — Rookie Detective: The second chapter continues after Malcolm’s murder. The recovered ledger points to a new death and a missing page.

answerKey.method: A staged fall from the archive stair after a sedative was slipped into Isaac’s coffee
answerKey.motive: To remove a ledger page showing modern dead drops hidden inside old hotel service codes
answerKey.keyEvidence: the scanner timestamp, archive-cart fibers, and the missing Page 47 tab
answerKey.explanation: Nora Vale is responsible. the scanner timestamp, archive-cart fibers, and the missing Page 47 tab proves the method and timeline. This chapter reveals: The ledger was not just history; it was a working codebook for present-day exchanges.

publicClues.title: Case File 2: The Missing Ledger
publicClues.text: Case File 2: The Missing Ledger begins inside The Tunnel. This is one chapter in a larger story, not a separate remake of the same mystery.

publicClues.title: The New Death
publicClues.text: The victim is Isaac Bell. The old Peacock Alley legend still matters, but this chapter adds a deeper layer to the ongoing investigation.

phone.title: Phone Evidence 1
phone.text: Isaac called Nora twice before reporting a missing page.

phone.title: Phone Evidence 2
phone.text: His final voicemail says: “Page 47 is not old accounting. It is directions.”

messages.title: Messages Evidence 1
messages.text: Nora texted Isaac: “Do not scan that page yet.”

messages.title: Messages Evidence 2
messages.text: A deleted message says: “The numbers still match the hallway marks.”

maps.title: Maps Evidence 1
maps.text: Isaac’s phone stops moving at the archive stair at 9:14 PM.

maps.title: Maps Evidence 2
maps.text: Nora’s keycard pings in the same stairwell three minutes earlier.

bank.title: Bank Evidence 1
bank.text: A small transfer posts to “Preservation Supplies” after the ledger scan.

bank.title: Bank Evidence 2
bank.text: The account description matches a dead drop label from Page 47.

photos.title: Photos Evidence 1
photos.text: A photo of the archive cart shows blue fibers caught in the wheel.

photos.title: Photos Evidence 2
photos.text: Nora’s scarf has the same blue weave visible in a later lobby photo.

social.title: Social Evidence 1
social.text: A historical post shows Page 47 cropped out of a preview image.

social.title: Social Evidence 2
social.text: The caption claims the page was “water damaged,” but the scan log says complete.

contacts.title: Contacts Evidence 1
contacts.text: Isaac saved Nora as “Archivist — do not trust scan order.”

contacts.title: Contacts Evidence 2
contacts.text: His contact note says: “Ask why 1224 repeats in 1927 and today.”

notes.title: Notes Evidence 1
notes.text: Detective note: Page 47 is the first proof that the ledger is being used, not merely collected.

notes.title: Notes Evidence 2
notes.text: Detective note: the fall looks accidental only if the sedative timeline is ignored.

files.title: Files Evidence 1
files.text: The scan batch jumps from Page 46 to Page 48.

files.title: Files Evidence 2
files.text: The scanner timestamp shows Page 47 was captured and deleted before police arrived.

browser.title: Browser Evidence 1
browser.text: A search asks how long a mild sedative remains detectable in black coffee.

browser.title: Browser Evidence 2
browser.text: Another search asks how to remove one page from a bound hotel register without tearing the spine.

openingNarration: Detectives, welcome to Case File 2: The Missing Ledger. Malcolm’s case proved the tunnel had been opened. Now the hotel register has gone missing from evidence, and archive courier Isaac Bell is dead near the service records. This chapter asks who edited the past after the first murder exposed the present.
revealScript: Nora Vale killed Isaac Bell after he found the missing ledger page and realized the old room-service codes were being reused as modern dead drops. Case File 2 proves The Tunnel mystery is no longer one murder. It is an active system hiding inside historic records.

A coincidence that does not affect the timeline

options.label: To cancel the speakeasy event

questions.prompt: Final Accusation: What larger truth does this chapter reveal?

options.label: The case is only about a confirmed historic romance
The ledger was not just history; it was a working codebook for present-day exchanges

The murder is unrelated to The Tunnel
